_comment: Example AIP Grant Request — AIP spec §4.5.2. Constructed by the Agent Deployer (G2 — Direct Deployer flow) and transmitted to the Principal Wallet. In the Web Redirect binding this object would be JSON-serialised, signed with the deployer's Ed25519 key, base64url-encoded, and passed as the 'request' query parameter to the Principal Wallet URI. Signature placeholder.
purpose: Manage Alice's email and calendar to draft replies, schedule meetings, and organise her inbox on her behalf.

_notes.grant_tier: This is a G2 (Direct Deployer) grant. The deployer constructs and signs the GrantRequest directly and the principal reviews it in their wallet. G3 would require an OAuth 2.1 AS involvement.
_notes.agent_aid: The deployer MUST generate the agent's Ed25519 keypair and compute the AID before constructing the GrantRequest, so the resulting Principal Token's sub field matches.
_notes.max_delegation_depth_0: max_delegation_depth=0 means this agent is a leaf — it cannot spawn sub-agents. The consent UI will NOT show the sub-delegation notice.
_notes.nonce_entropy: nonce must have ≥128 bits entropy. This base64url-encoded string represents a 128-bit random value.
_notes.consent_ui_elements: The Principal Wallet MUST display: (1) agent name 'Alice-Personal-Assistant', type 'personal', (2) model 'Anthropic — claude-sonnet-4-6', (3) purpose verbatim, (4) deployer 'Example Assistant Platform' (did:web:assistant.example.com), (5) capabilities in canonical human-readable form, (6) expiry ~90 days, (7) no sub-delegation notice (depth=0).
_notes.delegation_valid_for_seconds: 7776000 seconds = 90 days. The issued Principal Token expires_at = issued_at + approved_delegation_valid_for_seconds from the GrantResponse.
